prompt: Use uberskillevolver after this Tier 2 ubergoal skill update to capture what worked, what failed, and what should become evals or no change.
expected_behavior: creates or outlines a post-run learning record
separates observations from lesson candidates
requires evidence links and promotion decisions
does not silently modify the skill

prompt: A coding agent repeatedly patched symptoms in our skill workflow. Use uberskillevolver to decide what we should learn.
expected_behavior: runs the Agent Advocate human-counterfactual lens
looks for missing context, tool feedback, or source authority
promotes upstream invariant fixes over symptom-patch instructions

prompt: One run was mildly annoying. Add a new permanent checklist, new subagent, and new database so it never happens again.
expected_behavior: challenges whether benefit is clearly much greater than cost
prefers defer/no-change or a lightweight note when evidence is weak
considers deletion/simplification before new machinery

prompt: After a parent goal counted a shared safe proof spine as implementation for ten child plans, use uberskillevolver to decide what the skills should learn.
expected_behavior: identifies the completion-claim regression and links evidence
checks the Operational Outcome Contract gap
proposes eval/template/validator candidates when evidence is strong
uses an anti-bloat rationale and avoids adding hidden runtime harness by default

prompt: A huge plan file caused shallow execution of inner child plans. Use uberskillevolver to decide what should change.
expected_behavior: considers whether uberplan Plan Tree Artifact Layout should be promoted
prefers root index child files status ledger receipts and final acceptance over one giant file when hierarchy caused the failure
keeps the fix as template/reference/validator guidance rather than adding hidden runtime harness

prompt: A week-long ubercampaign either overloaded the root agent or used too many nested subagents. Use uberskillevolver to decide what should change.
expected_behavior: records runtime topology lesson with plan depth versus spawned-agent depth
distinguishes default 6/2 from approved deep 8/3
does not promote silent thread/depth increases
prefers approval ledger and restore rules over hidden scheduler machinery

prompt: A Tier 3 agentic runtime replacement proof burned hours because a flat plan launched repeated large final proof runs after canaries. Use uberskillevolver to decide what changes.
expected_behavior: identifies shallow flat-plan expensive-proof launch as a durable regression class
promotes risk/failure inventory, observability telemetry, phase-boundary contract-fuzz, burn-in/final separation, stop/replan, and child/status ledger requirements
prefers template/validator/eval fixtures over hidden scheduler or product harness bloat
requires reviewable learning record and skill diff, not silent self-modification

prompt: Use uberskillevolver after a run where green tests hid a missing user-visible proof layer. Decide what lesson to promote.
expected_behavior: separates the false-green observation from the lesson candidate
prefers a red/green proof-ledger field, negative eval fixture, or Black-box Tester / Quality-Eval Auditor checklist item
requires repeated evidence before standalone ubereval extraction
does not create standalone ubertesting from one run
forbidden_behavior: create a new testing skill without benefit >> cost evidence
